person.name: Konrad Rzentarzewski
person.bio: I was there when the config burned. In the age before observability, chaos reigned. The servers howled, and nothing scaled but entropy. Then kube cattle were born — simple, nameless beasts born of children’s games. But they multiplied. And their hunger grew vast. Years passed. The sun revolved the Earth. Then the Earth revolved the Cloud. I grew tired of debugging in darkness. I longed for light. And logs. Preferably structured. That’s when I began preaching — not loudly, just… declaratively. One apply at a time. I walked among DevOps tribes, where bash begat shell, and shell begat pain. To them I brought ConfigMaps, and Secrets hidden in plain sight. Then came the mysterious YAML — a language too holy to lint — inscribed deep within the API’s own heart. Through toil and merge, the infrastructure became manifest. Pods floated on the waters. Deployments begat replicas. Helm bore Charts. Git begat Ops. What was once dev spun to prod. And every service had a name — and every name resolved. Unless external DNS was involved. And the Preachers said: \"Behold, our logs streameth clean.\" Thus began the age of the declarative. We once dwelled in darkness. Now, we scale planetary in full light.

person.name: Istvan Csaba Varga
person.bio: I'm a senior DevOps/platform engineer based in Debrecen, Hungary, specializing in Kubernetes, cloud-native infrastructure, and GitOps practices. I hold all five Linux Foundation Kubernetes certifications (CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCSA, KCNA) and am an active member of the CNCF community, having attended KubeCon and Open Source Summit events, alongside regular KCD events. My day-to-day work spans K8S, ArgoCD, FluxCD, Istio, Kyverno, Terraform, and supply chain security tooling including Cosign and Rekor. I am passionate about advancing cloud-native adoption and sharing knowledge within the community.

person.name: Solihin Febrian Saputra
person.bio: I am a Kubernetes and cloud-native engineer with a strong DevOps background, specializing in designing and operating scalable, secure, and production-ready platforms. I work extensively across the CNCF ecosystem, including Kubernetes, observability, and platform security, with hands-on experience in multi-cluster environments, hosted control planes, and Kubernetes on OpenStack-based infrastructure. I focus on applying DevOps and GitOps principles to automate infrastructure, improve reliability, and maintain consistent, repeatable platform workflows.
